title: Teacher
subtitle: Certified Instructional
salaryRange: Salary and work year configurations are established in accordance with the current Collective Bargaining Agreement.
roleDescription: The Elementary Teacher facilitates student success and growth in academic and interpersonal skills by implementing district-approved curriculum, creating a safe and optimal learning environment, and documenting student progress to address the specific educational needs of individual learners.
roleHighlight: Ethics & Compliance Alignment: This position requires strict adherence to all Board Policies and Administrative Procedures, including Board Policy 5:120 regarding Employee Ethics, Conduct, and Conflict of Interest guidelines.
responsibilities: Develop and administer innovative curriculum consistent with school district goals and objectives
Promote a classroom environment that is safe, supportive, and highly conducive to student success
Design, translate, and implement personalized instruction to maximize available time for learning
Conduct ongoing assessments of student progress and modify instructional methods to fit individual student needs
Maintain familiarity with district, state, and national standardized assessments to maximize student achievement
Organize and maintain complete systems for accurate record-keeping, grading, and mandatory reporting profiles
Encourage parental involvement in students' education and ensure effective communication regarding milestones
Establish and maintain clear standards of student behavior to achieve a functional learning atmosphere
Communicate and collaborate effectively with other staff members and participate in building staff meetings
Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the classroom
requiredQuals: Valid Illinois State Board of Education Professional Educator Licensure (PEL) appropriate to assignment
Knowledge of current teaching methods, educational pedagogy, and differentiated instruction frameworks
Knowledge of applicable federal and state laws regarding education, safety, and students
preferredQuals: Excellent technology skills, including proficiency with data information networks and classroom software applications
Strong organizational skills with a demonstrated ability to coordinate schedules and manage timelines effectively
Demonstrated ability to establish and maintain cooperative working relationships with colleagues, parents, and community members
Commitment to ongoing professional growth by learning of current developments in the educational field
benefits: Medical, Dental, and Life Insurance Plans
Illinois Teachers' Retirement System (TRS) Pension Participation Framework
Optional Employee Contribution Access to 403(b) and 457 Retirement Accounts
Fixed Annual Allotments of Sick, Personal, and Family Leave Days
cultureText: Our instructional staff operates within a collaborative, team-oriented school community focused on student-centered growth and deeper learning. Physical demands include regularly sitting, standing, walking, traversing stairs, operating computers, and occasionally lifting or moving up to 20 pounds.
matchQuestions: Do you hold a valid Illinois Professional Educator Licensure (PEL) appropriate for an elementary setting?
Are you confident designing differentiated lesson plans that modify instruction for individual student learning styles?
Do you have experience managing classroom behavior and communicating student progress effectively with parents?
Are you comfortable collaborating on instructional action plans and participating in school staff meetings?
